headline: Visa Announces Global Expansion of Agentic Ready Program
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 29, 2026--Visa Inc. (NYSE: V) today announced the expansion of its global Agentic Ready program to clients in Asia Pacific and Latin America. First launched with banks and issuing partners across Europe, including the UK, Agentic Ready is designed to support the payments ecosystem as it prepares for the emergence of agent-led commerce.

content.title: Visa Announces Global Expansion of Agentic Ready Program
content.summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 29, 2026--Visa Inc. (NYSE: V) today announced the expansion of its global Agentic Ready program to clients in Asia Pacific and Latin America. First launched with banks and issuing partners across Europe, including the UK, Agentic Ready is designed to support the payments ecosystem as it prepares for the emergence of agent-led commerce.

headline: US stocks tick lower as oil prices keep spurting higher
summary: The U.S. stock market is ticking lower on Wednesday with the countdown to an afternoon announcement coming from the Federal Reserve on what it will do with interest rates.  Oil prices, meanwhile, continued to spurt higher because of the war with Iran.  The S&P 500 slipped 0.2%, a day after falling from its all-time high due to drops for artificial-intelligence stocks and worries about higher oil prices.

content.title: US stocks tick lower as oil prices keep spurting higher
content.summary: The U.S. stock market is ticking lower on Wednesday with the countdown to an afternoon announcement coming from the Federal Reserve on what it will do with interest rates. headline: Visa Stock Soars Because AI Agents Will Pay With Cards
summary: Visa stock leapt on Wednesday morning, leading the Dow Jones Industrial Average.  The surge came after it blew away fiscal second-quarter earnings forecasts late Tuesday on its strongest revenue growth since 2022.  The digital payment network, long seen as a potential casualty of fintech innovation, provided a shot of confidence that it will be a long-term winner in the AI era.

headline: Visa (V) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: In our fiscal second quarter, net revenue was up 17% year-over-year to $11.2 billion and EPS was up 20%.  This represented the strongest net revenue growth since 2022.  Payments volume grew 9% year-over-year in constant dollars to $3.7 trillion and processed transactions grew 9% year-over-year to $66 billion.

content.title: Visa (V) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
content.summary: In our fiscal second quarter, net revenue was up 17% year-over-year to $11.2 billion and EPS was up 20%.  This represented the strongest net revenue growth since 2022.  Payments volume grew 9% year-over-year in constant dollars to $3.7 trillion and processed transactions grew 9% year-over-year to $66 billion.

headline: US stocks drift in mixed trading as oil prices keep spurting higher
summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting in mixed trading Wednesday as the countdown ticks to an afternoon announcement from the Federal Reserve on what it will do with interest rates.  Oil prices, meanwhile, continued to spurt higher because of the war with Iran.  The S&P 500 slipped 0.1%, a day after falling from its all-time high due to drops for artificial-intelligence stocks and worries about higher oil prices.
